title: Coding-agent cross-session memory
scenario: A terminal-resident coding agent persists architectural decisions across days so it can recall "what we tried Tuesday" via pgvector similarity search.

title: Ephemeral test database for a risky migration
scenario: A coding agent provisions a throwaway Postgres in under a second, dry-runs a destructive migration, inspects the result, then discards the database — no production blast radius.

title: Repo-wide code dependency graph
scenario: An agent indexes a polyrepo into a blast-radius graph so it can answer "what breaks if I rename this function" before editing.

title: PR-review bot triggered by webhooks
scenario: A code-review agent receives PR webhooks from GitHub, posts inline comments, and re-reviews on each push while caching diffs between runs.

title: Sandboxed test runner per task
scenario: A coding agent spins up a throwaway container per task to run the user's test suite in isolation, tears it down on success or failure.

title: Tool-call rate-limit and budget cache
scenario: A coding agent caches LLM token budgets and rate-limit windows in Redis so parallel sub-agents don't burn quota fighting each other.

title: Conversation transcript archive
scenario: Coding sessions are persisted as JSONL transcripts for replay, fine-tuning, and "what did we try last sprint" search across the team.

title: Multi-repo shared scratchpad
scenario: Multiple agents working across separate repos coordinate via a shared scratchpad document store keyed by feature branch.

title: LangGraph state checkpoints
scenario: A LangGraph workflow checkpoints node state to Postgres so a crashed run resumes mid-graph instead of restarting from scratch.

title: CrewAI message bus fan-out
scenario: A crew of planner / retriever / critic agents publishes tasks on NATS subjects so any worker pod can pull and reply.

title: AutoGen group-chat history
scenario: An AutoGen multi-agent chat stores per-conversation message logs in Mongo for audit and replay across days.

title: Shared episodic memory store
scenario: A planner and a researcher agent read and write the same episodic memory table so each agent sees the other's findings.

title: Durable agent task queue
scenario: A supervisor agent enqueues sub-tasks on a durable queue; failed jobs re-emerge with exponential backoff.

title: Cross-framework A2A gateway
scenario: A gateway service translates A2A-protocol messages between a LangGraph crew and a CrewAI crew over pub/sub.

title: Live agent status broadcast
scenario: Worker agents broadcast heartbeats on a status subject so a dashboard shows which agent is stuck mid-tool-call.

title: Clinical-scribe note storage
scenario: A medical scribe agent transcribes doctor-patient visits and stores structured SOAP notes per encounter with auditable history.

title: Personal-injury demand letters
scenario: A legal agent drafts demand letters from case files, stores prior versions, and emits webhooks to the firm's case-management system.

title: AML transaction monitor
scenario: A finance agent ingests transaction streams, flags suspicious patterns, and persists compliance decisions with full reasoning trace.

title: Adaptive-tutoring student model
scenario: A tutor agent tracks per-student concept mastery, picks the next problem, and adjusts difficulty based on response patterns.

title: Contract redline cache
scenario: A contract-review agent caches clause embeddings so re-running redlines on a 200-page MSA is instant on the second pass.

title: EHR appointment webhook fan-in
scenario: A medical-intake agent receives appointment-created webhooks from multiple EHR vendors and unifies them into one queue.

title: Obsidian-vault embedding sync
scenario: A personal research agent embeds Obsidian notes nightly into pgvector and answers questions across years of writing.

title: Cross-device chat history
scenario: A personal assistant stores conversation history in Mongo so the same agent picks up context on phone, laptop, and watch.

title: Daily-journal episodic memory
scenario: An assistant logs each day as a structured journal entry and retrieves "what was I working on Monday?" with sub-second latency.

title: arXiv-and-RSS research feed
scenario: A research agent receives webhook pings from arXiv and RSS bridges, dedupes via Redis, and stores PDFs for later retrieval.

title: Voice-memo capture pipeline
scenario: A second-brain agent receives uploaded voice memos, transcribes them, and files transcripts plus audio for semantic search.

title: CRM for one person
scenario: A personal CRM agent remembers names, birthdays, and last-conversation summaries indexed per contact.

title: Browser-session cookie store
scenario: A browser agent persists login cookies and session state per target site so it doesn't re-auth on every run.

title: Scraped product-price history
scenario: A shopping-watcher agent scrapes prices hourly and stores time-series rows for "alert me when it drops 20%".

title: Screenshot evidence archive
scenario: A QA agent captures before/after screenshots on every test run and stores them keyed by run-id for diff review.

title: Browser job queue with retries
scenario: A fleet of Playwright workers pulls navigation tasks from a queue, marks them done, and retries on captcha.

title: Form-fill state machine
scenario: A long-running form-completion agent persists field-by-field progress so a captcha pause doesn't lose 30 minutes of work.

title: Accessibility-tree selector cache
scenario: A scraping agent caches a11y-tree snapshots in Redis so repeat selectors resolve in single-digit milliseconds.

title: Full dev backend in one curl
scenario: An AI agent or developer provisions Postgres + Redis + MongoDB anonymously to develop against — no Docker, no cloud account, no installer — and tears it down when done.

title: Pre-commit skill-scanner webhook
scenario: An MCP skill security scanner receives pre-commit webhooks, scans agent skills against a ruleset, and blocks the push on critical findings.

title: SARIF scan-result store
scenario: A security scanner posts SARIF results into Postgres so trend dashboards show drift over weeks of commits.

title: Deploy-status MCP server
scenario: An MCP server tracks live deploy status across environments and pushes updates to a chat agent over pub/sub.

title: CI flake-tracker
scenario: A CI bot ingests test-run webhooks, fingerprints failures, and surfaces "this test is flaky 30% of runs".

title: High-volume PR-review pipeline
scenario: An automated reviewer handles thousands of MRs/day, queues each review job, and stores comment artifacts per run.

title: On-call incident-response agent
scenario: An on-call agent listens for alert webhooks, executes a runbook, and posts the action log back into the incident ticket.

title: A2A agent-card registry
scenario: A marketplace stores Agent Cards (skills, tags, pricing) and serves discovery queries to client agents.

title: x402 micropayment ledger
scenario: An agent-payment hub records x402 micropayments between agents and reconciles balances per principal.

title: Agent reputation log
scenario: Buyer agents leave ratings on seller agents after each tool call; reputation scores aggregate hourly.

title: AP2 mandate audit trail
scenario: An agentic-commerce gateway stores signed user mandates ("buy X up to $Y") for later dispute resolution.

title: Cross-agent shared inbox
scenario: Two agents negotiate over a shared subject; messages persist in JetStream so neither needs to be online simultaneously.

title: Agent-marketplace preview thumbnails
scenario: An agent marketplace stores screenshot previews of each listed agent's UI for human browsing.

title: One-afternoon MVP backend
scenario: A solo founder spins up Postgres + Redis from a curl call in their Claude Code session and ships a paid product by evening.

title: Pre-launch waitlist store
scenario: A landing page captures emails via a webhook receiver and stores them with UTM context for nurture campaigns.

title: Stripe-event entitlements
scenario: A SaaS receives Stripe webhooks, updates user plan tier, and invalidates a Redis entitlement cache atomically.

title: Solo-founder analytics warehouse
scenario: A founder pipes product events into Postgres and runs SQL queries via an MCP-Postgres tool from chat.

title: Side-project container deploy
scenario: An indie hacker ships a Dockerized app to a subdomain with one HTTP call — no DevOps account, no Helm chart, no IaC.

title: 24-hour hackathon backend
scenario: A team provisions Postgres + Mongo + MinIO anonymously, ships their demo, and lets the stack expire 24 hours after judging.

title: Classroom-per-student sandbox
scenario: A CS professor provisions one ephemeral Postgres per student for a SQL assignment, dropped after grading.

title: Agent-resilience chaos lab
scenario: A research hackathon tests how agents behave when their database, cache, and message bus randomly fail mid-task.

_comment: Adding a new use case = one entry above. The dashboard's UseCasesPage groups by category and renders services as tags. Service codes: pg, redis, mongo, nats, minio, webhook, deploy.
